### Fix 2
Line: 65
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuToggle> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuToggle> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuToggle variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuToggle> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuToggle><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuToggle>

Incident context:
  componentName: MenuToggle
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Select

Prop: icon

Rule [sd-test-select-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Select is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Select renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Select may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.

Let me start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1 Analysis (Line 75 & 80 - Select prop changes)

**Line 75**: `popperProps={{ position: 'right' }}` — The `popperProps` type changed from `SelectPopperProps` to `PopperOptions`. The `position` field name may have changed. Looking at the PF6 `PopperOptions`, the `position` property should still work but let me check the incident details more carefully. The incident says `propObjectValues: ["right"]` and is about `popperProps`. The type changed from `SelectPopperProps` to `PopperOptions`. In PF6's `PopperOptions`, the equivalent of `position` is `placement` or it's kept as `position`. Actually, looking at PF6 PopperOptions, position mapping isn't explicitly listed as a mapping — the type just changed. The `position` property should still be valid in `PopperOptions`. I'll keep it as-is since the type change is structural but the property name `position` is common to both.

Wait, let me re-read: the incident context says the `popperProps` property values include `"right"`. In PF5 `SelectPopperProps`, `position` accepted string values like `'right'`. In PF6 `PopperOptions`, the equivalent might be different. But the fix strategy just says `SelectPopperProps -> PopperOptions` mapping. Since there's no specific value mapping, I should leave the structure as-is — the type annotation changes but the runtime value is the same.

**Line 80**: `onSelect={(event, value) => value && onSelect(value as FilterDir, event)}` — The `value` parameter type changed from `string | number` to `SelectOptionProps['value']`. The cast `value as FilterDir` should still work since `FilterDir` is a string type and `SelectOptionProps['value']` includes strings. No code change needed for the callback itself.

Both of these are already using the v6 `Select` composition (SelectList, SelectOption are already imported and used). The code is already migrated. No changes needed for Fix 1.

### Fix 2 Analysis (Line 65 - MenuToggle icon)

Line 65-73: The `MenuToggle` contains `<FilterIcon />` as a child with `variant="plain"`. Per the fix, icon-only content should be moved to the `icon` prop:

```jsx
// Before:
<MenuToggle ... variant="plain">
  <FilterIcon />
</MenuToggle>

// After:
<MenuToggle ... variant="plain" icon={<FilterIcon />} />
```

### Fix 3 Analysis (Line 1 - Test behavioral changes)

This is about behavioral changes in Select's sub-components (Popper's `appendTo` default changed). This is informational for test files. The main source file doesn't need changes — this is a test-awareness note. No code changes needed in this file.

Now let me check for companion test files:

No companion test files for this specific component. Now let me apply the changes.

**Fix 1**: The code already uses v6 Select composition (SelectList, SelectOption imported and used correctly). The `popperProps` and `onSelect` type changes are structural type annotation changes — the runtime code doesn't need modification. Already applied / no change needed.

**Fix 2**: The `<MenuToggle>` on lines 65-73 has `<FilterIcon />` as its only child with `variant="plain"`. Must move `FilterIcon` to the `icon` prop and make `MenuToggle` self-closing.

**Fix 3**: Informational about test behavioral changes. No source code change needed in this file, and no test files exist for this component.

content.text: Let me verify the final result:

The file looks correct. All imports are still used (`FilterIcon` is now referenced in the `icon` prop), and the structure is clean.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — Select family migration (`popperProps` type change, `onSelect` type change)
**Already applied / no change needed.** The file already uses the v6 `Select` composition pattern (`Select` → `SelectList` → `SelectOption`), with all three components imported from `@patternfly/react-core`. The `popperProps` and `onSelect` props are already structurally compatible with v6 types — the `popperProps` object `{ position: 'right' }` is valid for `PopperOptions`, and the `onSelect` callback signature (`(event, value) => ...`) is compatible with the new `SelectOptionProps['value']` parameter type since `FilterDir` is a string type.

### Fix 2 — MenuToggle icon children-to-prop migration
**Applied.** Moved `<FilterIcon />` from children of `<MenuToggle>` to the `icon` prop (`icon={<FilterIcon />}`), and made `<MenuToggle>` self-closing. This follows the PF v6 pattern where icon-only `MenuToggle` components with `variant="plain"` should use the `icon` prop instead of children.

### Fix 3 — Select transitive behavioral changes (Popper `appendTo` default)
**No change needed.** This is an informational rule about behavioral changes in Select's rendered sub-component (Popper's `appendTo` default changed from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`). It affects test files, not source files. No companion test files exist for this component (`summary-filter-button.tsx`), so no test updates are required.

The file looks correct. All imports are still used (`FilterIcon` is now referenced in the `icon` prop), and the structure is clean.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — Select family migration (`popperProps` type change, `onSelect` type change)
**Already applied / no change needed.** The file already uses the v6 `Select` composition pattern (`Select` → `SelectList` → `SelectOption`), with all three components imported from `@patternfly/react-core`. The `popperProps` and `onSelect` props are already structurally compatible with v6 types — the `popperProps` object `{ position: 'right' }` is valid for `PopperOptions`, and the `onSelect` callback signature (`(event, value) => ...`) is compatible with the new `SelectOptionProps['value']` parameter type since `FilterDir` is a string type.

### Fix 2 — MenuToggle icon children-to-prop migration
**Applied.** Moved `<FilterIcon />` from children of `<MenuToggle>` to the `icon` prop (`icon={<FilterIcon />}`), and made `<MenuToggle>` self-closing. This follows the PF v6 pattern where icon-only `MenuToggle` components with `variant="plain"` should use the `icon` prop instead of children.

### Fix 3 — Select transitive behavioral changes (Popper `appendTo` default)
**No change needed.** This is an informational rule about behavioral changes in Select's rendered sub-component (Popper's `appendTo` default changed from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`). It affects test files, not source files. No companion test files exist for this component (`summary-filter-button.tsx`), so no test updates are required.
